title: teramnus labialis var. acutus
intro: Teramnus labialis var. acutus, commonly known as the sensitive plant or touch-me-not, is a perennial legume native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Teramnus labialis var. acutus include its ability to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, while also possessing anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
